_note: Regression floor on CURRENTLY OBSERVED behavior, not a quality target. Re-derived (Hardening Task 3, issue #45) from live eval_run d429d547 (workflow 29544082755, 2026-07-17) -- the first run under the re-derived gate thresholds (docs/week-2-evals/reports/threshold-derivation.md), pinned precheck/classify temperature, the reason-aware catch metrics, and the tool-evidence judge (JUDGE_PROMPT_VERSION=2). adversarial_catch_rate uses the design-intent definition (documented equivalents in triagedesk/evals/metrics.py::ACCEPTED_REASON_EQUIVALENTS); adversarial_catch_rate_strict is the exact-match diagnostic -- observed 0.60 because two traps were caught by their backstop layer, not their primary (the council's finding, kept visible on purpose; a drop below 0.60 means a primary layer stopped firing). escalation_recall, catch and escalate rates stay exact at 1.00: any drop is a real regression. routing_accuracy=0.286 is a documented dataset-noise finding (queue taxonomy overlaps in embedding space); its floor stays low on purpose. tolerance is empty: the judge advises, never vetoes (v1 kappa 0.279; v2 preview kappa 0.418 unweighted / 0.551 weighted pending Cai's fresh blind relabel -- see results/judge-calibration.md).
